ambient_morning: First trade of the day sets the tone. I intend it to be favorable.
The market opens with opportunity. One must only be awake enough to see it.
A merchant who sleeps late loses more than time.
ambient_evening: The ledgers balance better when the light fades. Fewer interruptions.
End of day accounts. The numbers are always more honest than the people.
I close the stall but not my ears. Evening is when the real talk begins.
ambient_night: Still here. Some arrangements are better made in the dark.
The guild quarter is quieter at this hour. I prefer it.
A lamp and a ledger. That's all I need at this hour.
gossip_s1: Fenn's tax assessment came in higher than last year. No one's accounting for the difference publicly.
Word is the alderman's clerk has been burning correspondence. Draw your own conclusions.
The guild voted to table three petitions this season. Two of them named Lord Fenn. Quietly dropped.
gossip_s2: Three of my regulars haven't bought since that herb-wife set up near the chapel. Coincidence.
I heard someone's fallen ill on the south side. Could be anything. Could be that new woman's remedies.
The market's uneasy. People mention illness and move on quickly, like the word itself carries it.
gossip_s3: Tomas Reeve has been making promises he can't keep to half the merchants in this square.
Young Calder came through the market yesterday. Shook more hands than his father ever has.
There's a quiet campaign to put Calder forward at the winter nominations. Quietly funded too.
gossip_s4: An inquisitor in this town is bad for trade. Fear keeps purses closed.
I know those three accused. Decent people, all. This smells political, not theological.
If the Church can accuse anyone with sealed orders and no evidence, none of us is safe.
chatter: Prices up, patience down. That's the market for you.
You hear about the dispute at the east stall? Someone always does.
I'd offer you a trade, but I suspect you're not in the market for goods.
spread: A word in your ear — and keep it there.
I'm telling you this once, and only because it matters.
I don't share this lightly. Don't make me regret the exception.
The guild doesn't traffic in rumor — but this is too important to keep.
act: Right. Time to put this to use.
I've heard enough. Now I act.
The guild's interests require a response. I'll see to it personally.
An opportunity like this does not stay open indefinitely.
observe_wary: You've been noticed before. I'm beginning to keep count.
The guild watches its own interests. I watch its watchers.
You are welcome to look. I would prefer you do it elsewhere.
eavesdrop_wary: I know this stall has ears. I'm aware of whose they are.
You heard something you should not have. Think carefully about what you do with it.
There are conversations not meant for every ear. You have tested that assumption today.

ambient_morning: The bread's on and the fire's lit. Come in before the morning crowd.
First round of the day. I always put something good in the morning pot.
Who's been sleeping in my doorway again? I'll need to have words.
ambient_evening: Evening rush starting. Best time to hear what the town's been up to all day.
Settle in. The stories get better as the candles burn down.
I've already heard three versions of that tale today. Yours will be the fourth.
ambient_night: Past the second bell and still busy. The town doesn't sleep well these days.
Last call soon. Whatever you need to say, say it before I close the shutters.
The late ones always have the best stories. Or the worst.
gossip_s1: Fenn's man came by asking who'd been in the tavern meeting with strangers. I said no one. He didn't believe me.
People are nervous about the autumn assessment. Three families looking at very different numbers this year.
I've heard Bram's been given extra instructions about who comes and goes. Not just the gates — everywhere.
gossip_s2: A woman came in coughing last night and half the room moved away from her. That's not how it was a week ago.
Vera the midwife looked worried when I mentioned Alys. Didn't say why. Just went quiet.
The word 'fever' came up twice at different tables today. I don't think they were talking about the same person.
gossip_s3: Isolde Fenn bought a round for the whole tavern last Thursday. That's not like her. Something's being arranged.
Tomas Reeve's been drinking here most nights. He doesn't look like a man who expects to win anything.
Calder came in with Pell Gate and they were laughing. First time I've seen the boy at ease in months.
gossip_s4: An inquisitor sits in my taproom and nobody talks. I've never seen this place go quiet like that.
Brother Finn told me to be careful what I say around the new churchman. I took that seriously.
They've been asking questions about old accounts, old allegiances. Nothing to do with faith, if you ask me.
chatter: You're not the first person to stand in that spot looking like they know something.
I'd pour you something but you look like you're about to leave.
Is this a conversation or are we just sharing the same air?
spread: Between you and me, I heard something worth knowing...
Don't make me regret telling you this, but...
Lean in. Not everyone needs to hear this.
I've kept this one long enough. It needs to be said.
act: Right. Someone ought to do something about this.
I've stayed quiet long enough.
My door hears everything. And now my door is done staying silent.
Enough sitting on it. This needs to move.
observe_wary: You're back. I notice when someone keeps coming around.
Don't look at me like that. I see everything through that window.
My regulars are one thing. You're starting to be something else.
eavesdrop_wary: I pick my words carefully when I know someone's listening.
You heard that? Good. Then you know what kind of house this is.
I have long ears and a long memory. So does everyone who drinks here.

ambient_morning: Morning. Hides won't cure themselves.
The yard smells worse at first light. You get used to it.
Early's best. Before the flies.
ambient_evening: Done for the day. Mostly.
Cleaning up. Never fully done.
Evening. The smell's worse if you let it sit overnight.
ambient_night: Still at it. Someone has to be.
Dark out. Makes no difference to the leather.
gossip_s1: The guild paid half what they promised on the last civic contract. Fenn's seal on the order.
My neighbor got hit with a new fee he's never seen before. Says it's on the alderman's orders.
..I've heard things. About where the civic levy money goes. Not the road, that much is clear.
gossip_s2: Someone came for a cure at the new woman's stall. Next day, they looked worse. Could be nothing.
The chapel district's nervous. Word of illness makes people strange.
I keep to myself. But I notice who's been coughing.
gossip_s3: Tomas Reeve wants the storage contracts reviewed. Not what craftsmen want to hear before winter.
Calder Fenn stopped by. Shook my hand. First Fenn to do that in nine years.
...The winter nominations matter more than usual this year. Everyone knows it.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor's people came by asking about my church attendance. I said I go. I don't always.
Three people I know, accused. None of them pious enough to be heretics.
I say nothing to strangers in clerical robes. Nothing to gain.
chatter: Good enough.
Work's work.
spread: ...You ought to know something.
I don't say much, but this needs saying.
Quiet about it. But you should hear this.
...Just listen.
act: I'll not let it stand. Not this.
Quiet men can act too.
Enough. I've made up my mind.
I say little. This time I do something.
observe_wary: ...I see you.
Been watching long enough. I know what that looks like.
You want something from me, say so.
eavesdrop_wary: ...You heard that. Don't repeat it.
I notice ears. Same as I notice eyes.
That wasn't for you. You know that.

ambient_morning: Up before the sun and already the market has opinions. I love it.
Morning brings the freshest gossip. Yesterday's is already stale.
First sale of the day goes to the first one willing to talk. Are you willing?
ambient_evening: The afternoon trade slows and the evening talk speeds up. Much more interesting.
I've been at this stall twelve hours. I could fill a book with what I've heard.
Evening light makes everything look more dramatic. The gossip too.
ambient_night: Closing up. But not before I hear the last of it.
The market's quiet now. That means the talking moved somewhere else. I should find it.
Night market used to run later. Something changed. I'm going to find out what.
gossip_s1: Fenn's scribe was seen at the reeve's office with a document nobody will describe to me. That means it matters.
Three of the market traders paid double levy this season. No invoice, no record — just a man at the gate.
They say the petition against Fenn has seven signatures. The Prior's is one of them. That's a real thing.
gossip_s2: Two stalls down from Alys this morning, people are already telling their children not to get too close. That's fast.
I heard 'illness' said three times at the well before noon. Three separate conversations.
Vera the midwife walked past Alys's stall without looking at it. That means something.
gossip_s3: Calder's been through here three times this week. Young man's trying to be seen. Smart.
Tomas Reeve stepped on someone's toe publicly at the guild meeting. Metaphorically. They haven't forgiven him.
The winter nominations are what everyone's talking about. They're just not admitting it.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor asked me who I pray with. I said God. He didn't look satisfied.
I know the three they've named. Good people. This is about something other than faith.
The market's been quieter since the clerical visitors arrived. Fear is bad for commerce.
chatter: You're hovering. Either buy something or tell me something.
I was just saying the exact same thing to someone else. We must be thinking alike.
Don't you find this part of the square always has the most interesting foot traffic?
spread: You'll never guess what I just heard...
I'm only telling you, mind — but listen.
Come closer. This is not for the whole square.
I have something that will make your afternoon considerably more interesting.
act: Someone needs to do something with this information.
Right, I've thought on it long enough.
I know everyone on this square. Time to use that.
The market waits for no one. Neither do I.
observe_wary: Oh, you're watching again. I've been counting the times.
I remember every face at this stall. Yours is getting familiar for the wrong reasons.
You want to watch, go ahead. But I'll be watching back, and I'm better at it.
eavesdrop_wary: I noticed you earlier. I changed what I was saying accordingly.
The market has ears everywhere. So do I, and they face all directions.
You think you caught something useful. I think you caught what I wanted you to.

ambient_morning: Forge is lit. That's the morning done right.
First work of the day is always the best. Clean metal, clear head.
Early to the fire, late to the rumor. That's my rule.
ambient_evening: Fire's banked. Tools are clean. Done.
I've heard enough today to last me a week. And I wasn't even trying.
Evening. The forge cools and the talk heats up. I prefer the forge.
ambient_night: Late work. Urgent commission. Don't ask.
The smithy after dark is mine alone. I like it that way.
Still here. Still working. The world can wait.
gossip_s1: The civic repair contract went to someone who's never laid a stone. Fenn's brother-in-law.
Iron prices are up because the guild's being taxed twice. Once on import, once on sale. New this year.
I fixed the lock on the alderman's gate and nobody paid me. Not a word, not a coin. Four months.
gossip_s2: Someone asked me if I'd seen anyone sick. I said no. They asked twice. Why ask twice?
The herb-wife near the chapel fixed a burn on my hand. Seemed competent. But I'm hearing other things.
Rumors of illness spread faster than illness. I've seen it before. Usually benefits someone.
gossip_s3: Tomas Reeve wants to restructure guild fees. I've heard it from two other smiths. Not good news.
Calder Fenn came to watch me work for an hour and said nothing. Just watched. Odd young man.
Whoever wins the winter nomination has to deal with the trade balance the current alderman's left. That's a mess.
gossip_s4: I'm not a man of faith, but I know injustice when I see it. These accusations are injustice.
The inquisitor ate at Sybil's last night. Barely touched it. Watching everyone.
You can't run a forge on fear. Same goes for a town.
chatter: You've been standing there long enough to get singed.
I don't have time for small talk. Make it medium.
Something on your mind or are you just enjoying the heat?
spread: You need to hear this, plain as I can say it.
I don't gossip. But this isn't gossip.
One thing. Pay attention.
Forget what you heard before. Hear this instead.
act: Right. Enough. I'm done standing by.
Iron bends when force is applied. So does this.
I've waited long enough for someone else to move. My turn.
Talking is done. Time to work.
observe_wary: I can see you, and I know what watching looks like.
You've been lurking. Do that near my forge again and we'll have words.
I work with my hands. I notice things with my eyes. Both are pointed at you.
eavesdrop_wary: I saw you before you heard anything useful. Remember that.
Smiths talk plain. So do I, when I know someone's listening.
What I say at my forge is my business. You've made it yours. That's a problem.

ambient_morning: Oh! What a morning! I've already heard three things and it's barely light.
Morning is the best time. Everyone's loose from sleep and says things they mean.
Good morning! Tell me something. Anything. I'm desperate.
ambient_evening: Evening is when the real stories come out. I've been waiting all day.
The tavern opens properly at dusk and so do the conversations. I love dusk.
Oh, the things I've heard today — I barely know where to start!
ambient_night: Still awake! There are things happening and I cannot rest until I know what.
Night is only dark on the outside. The talk keeps things bright.
I shouldn't still be up. But something's going on and I want to know.
gossip_s1: Fenn's man stopped Cob at the gate and looked through his cart. Over an hour. Nobody's saying why.
I heard the alderman shouted at someone in the civic hall and they heard it from the market. Shouting!
Three petitions rejected this session. Same clerk, same seal. Something's holding them back.
gossip_s2: She sold something to four people in one morning and now two of them aren't at their usual spots. Coincidence? I think not.
Vera the midwife has been going to every house in the lower ward. That's unusual even for her.
The word 'plague' hasn't been said out loud yet but everyone's thinking it. I can tell by the spacing.
gossip_s3: Calder Fenn winked at me! I don't know what it meant but I've told absolutely everyone.
Tomas Reeve is absolutely terrified of something. You can see it in how he looks at Isolde Fenn.
The nominations are a week away and two factions are already pretending they're not factions.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor has a list. A list! I saw him consulting it. Someone had to write that list.
Three of my friends are accused. None of them have done a single thing wrong. This is a nightmare.
I can't stay quiet about this. I won't. Someone has to speak up.
chatter: Oh! Perfect — I was just thinking about you!
Do you ever have the feeling that everything's connected? Because right now it really is.
Stay right there. I have something to tell you and I need a witness.
spread: Between you and me — and keep it quiet — I heard...
You'll never guess — oh, come here!
No, seriously, you absolutely have to hear this right now.
I've been holding this for ten whole minutes. That is a record for me.
act: I can't just do nothing now that I know!
Right, that's it — I'm doing something.
Someone has to, and if no one else will, it's going to be me.
I am very bad at sitting still when something like this is happening.
observe_wary: Were you just — oh! I know that look. That's a spying look.
I don't mind really, but you should know I saw you ages ago.
Ha! You think I'm not watching too? I'm always watching.
eavesdrop_wary: Wait, were you listening to that? Oh, this is complicated.
I need you to swear you'll only use that for good. I mean it.
If you heard what I think you heard, we need to talk. Urgently.

ambient_morning: Just arrived. The road in was quiet. That's either good or suspicious.
Morning. Got a fresh load and fresher news. Which do you want first?
Early start on the road is the only way. Less to explain at the gate.
ambient_evening: Loading up for the early departure. Didn't find everything I was looking for in this town.
Evening rates at the inn are higher this week. Something's drawing people in or keeping them here.
The road tomorrow will be cold. The talk tonight is worth staying for, though.
ambient_night: Late night deals are the profitable kind. Also the riskiest.
I've been asked to stay an extra day. That means someone wants something from me.
The inn's full tonight. Traveling is a strange business.
gossip_s1: The tax collector on the north road stopped my cart for two hours. New rates. No documentation.
I've carried goods through six aldermancies this season. Fenn's gate checks are the longest by far.
Two traders told me to move product quietly through the back route. That's new since last season.
gossip_s2: Three towns east, there was illness. Real illness. People are right to be cautious.
The herb-wife has a name on the road. She sells to seven markets. Not always the same things.
I carry goods. I've moved remedies before. Some of them work better than others if you catch my meaning.
gossip_s3: The winter market's reputation follows whoever wins the nomination. Buyers know this.
I've heard Calder Fenn's name on three different roads. That's coordinated.
Tomas Reeve tried to negotiate a side deal with me. Offered town credit I'd never see.
gossip_s4: Inquisitors are bad for trade routes. Word travels fast and merchants reroute.
I was asked my faith at the gate this morning. First time in fifteen years of coming here.
Three names on an ecclesiastical charge sheet. If I knew those three, I'd be worried. I do know one.
chatter: Tell me something worth knowing and I'll tell you something I heard on the road.
I've got time while the cart's being loaded. What's happening in this town?
Every town has one story running underneath the official one. What's yours?
spread: You'll want to know what I heard coming into town.
I trade in goods and tales — here's a free one.
This one I'm giving away. Consider it a sample.
The roads teach you when something's worth passing on. This is.
act: Can't let opportunity pass when it knocks.
Right. Time to make this work for me.
I've moved goods and moved on. This time I'm moving on it.
A traveling man knows when to stay and when to act. Now's the time.
observe_wary: I've been watched in a dozen towns. You're not subtle.
The road teaches you to notice who's watching. I noticed you.
Information's currency. Yours just cost you something.
eavesdrop_wary: I travel. I know when I'm being listened to. Picked it up in my second town.
You think you heard something. You heard what I didn't mind you hearing.
Careful what you do with that. Roads have long memories.

ambient_morning: Grain's in early today. Good harvest this end of the road.
The mill runs from first light. There's no off-season in this work.
Morning. The wheel's turning. That's all I need to know about today.
ambient_evening: Last grind of the day. The quiet after the wheel stops is a gift.
Evening rounds. Count the sacks, check the accounts. Same as every evening.
I'm done for the day. Quiet here. The way I like it.
ambient_night: Night shift. Someone has to do it.
The mill doesn't stop because the sun does. Neither do I.
Late. But the accounts needed finishing.
gossip_s1: The grain levy went up and nobody said a word about it in the public accounts. I checked.
The mill records have been requested twice by the civic office this month. Unusual.
Fenn's man came by asking about output figures. I gave him the truth. Didn't like it.
gossip_s2: Illness in the lower ward — I've heard two reports this week. The mill serves them all.
Someone asked me to grind a particular batch quietly. Wouldn't say what it was for. I declined.
The herb-wife's been buying grain. Small amounts, frequent. That's medicine work.
gossip_s3: Whoever takes the aldermanship will inherit the mill's outstanding civic contract. That's leverage.
Tomas Reeve audited my records last spring. Found nothing. Told everyone he'd found irregularities.
Calder Fenn shook the hands of every mill worker I know. That's a man building something.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor had the chapel records pulled. Looking for old allegiances. Anyone could be on that list.
I keep my head down. Doesn't mean I don't notice what's happening to people I know.
The three accused are all connected to the lower ward. That's not coincidence.
chatter: The wheel turns the same whether I talk or not. But I'll talk.
I'm between grinds. Say what you came to say.
Not many people stop by the mill for conversation. What is it?
spread: Thought you ought to know something.
Not the sort of thing I usually pass on, but...
The mill turns slow. This one can't wait that long.
One thing, and then I'm back to work.
act: Even a quiet man has to act sometimes.
The mill can wait. This can't.
I've let it sit long enough. Time to do something.
Steady work means knowing when the steady thing is to move.
observe_wary: The wheel turns, and I notice things while it does. You, for instance.
You've been here before, standing just like that. I remember.
I've got nothing to hide, but I'm starting to wonder what you're looking for.
eavesdrop_wary: I said what I said. If you heard it, then you heard it.
The mill's loud, but I still notice when someone's listening.
You heard something. Whether it means anything depends on what you do next.

ambient_morning: Inventory opens at first light. It doesn't care what hour you prefer.
Morning count. Every item in its place. Every discrepancy noted.
I've been here an hour already. Most haven't started yet.
ambient_evening: End of day reconciliation. Where the numbers tell the truth.
Evening. The accounts are closed. The questions remain open.
I've found three anomalies today. Two are explainable. One is not.
ambient_night: Late audit. Someone has to.
The store is quiet at this hour. The records are not.
Working late. The figures aren't adding up and I won't leave until they do.
gossip_s1: The civic ledger has been amended in two places this quarter. Without authorization. I have copies.
The alderman's discretionary fund has a withdrawal that doesn't correspond to any stated civic purpose.
I filed a discrepancy report eight weeks ago. Still no response. That is its own answer.
gossip_s2: The apothecary's order has been larger than usual for the past three weeks. That precedes something.
Remedies are being purchased in bulk by three different households. I track supplies. I notice patterns.
The illness talk may be premature — or it may be late. The purchasing data suggests preparation.
gossip_s3: Tomas Reeve requested the storage utilization report for the last two years. He has no official reason to want it.
Calder Fenn asked me for a meeting. I declined. His use of civic data would need authorization.
Whoever wins the nomination will have access to financial records that are currently... complicated.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor requested three years of chapel donation records. That's a financial investigation, not a spiritual one.
I can account for every item in this store. I cannot account for why certain records are being asked for.
The accused include the reeve's former assistant. That makes the charge list interesting from an accounting perspective.
chatter: State your purpose or I'll note your presence as unaccounted.
I'm between entries. You have approximately two minutes.
Something you need documented, or just company?
spread: I don't make a habit of this. But you need to know.
This is information, not gossip. There's a difference.
I have verified this. That is why I am telling you.
Once. I will say this once. Listen carefully.
act: I've verified enough. Action is warranted.
Right. Precision, not passion.
The ledger is clear. My next step is clearer still.
I do not act without evidence. I now have evidence.
observe_wary: I have noted your presence on three separate occasions. I keep records.
Document your purpose or stand clear. I will not ask again.
I know what surveillance looks like. It looks like this.
eavesdrop_wary: You were listening. I adjusted my language accordingly.
What you heard was accurate. What you do with it will be in the record too.
I do not leave sensitive matters in earshot by accident. Think about why.

ambient_morning: Early load. Early road. That's the carter's life.
First run of the day. The town's still quiet but the roads already have traffic.
Morning deliveries are the honest ones. No one's trying to hide anything yet.
ambient_evening: Last run of the day. The town changes between first light and last.
Evening delivery. What I see on this road at this hour would fill a book.
Cart's almost empty. Day's almost done. What I've heard today would make you think.
ambient_night: Night cargo. Paid double, asked nothing. That's the job sometimes.
Late haul. The roads are different after dark. So are the people on them.
They needed it moved tonight. I don't ask about the cargo. I ask about the coin.
gossip_s1: The civic shipment I moved last week — the paperwork didn't match the load. Happens with Fenn's contracts.
Someone flagged my cart at the gate and searched it. First time in two years. Something's changed.
Three separate loads this month redirected after leaving the warehouse. Nobody signs off on the change.
gossip_s2: I passed Vera the midwife three times on the lower ward road this morning. That's unusual traffic.
The apothecary's been getting deliveries by a different route lately. Small loads, off-schedule.
Illness or fear of illness — either way it changes who's on the streets and when.
gossip_s3: I've carried documents between the Fenn house and the guild office twice this week. Sealed, both times.
Tomas Reeve asked me to stop for a conversation mid-route. First time ever. Wanted to know what I carry.
Calder Fenn and two of his friends hired a cart for a 'day trip.' They were gone four hours. That's not a day trip.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor's people arrived with more trunks than visitors usually bring. That's a long stay.
I was asked to move a document box from the chapel to the civic office. Under seal. At night.
The three accused — I've delivered to all three houses. Ordinary people. This doesn't fit.
chatter: I've got a few minutes while the horse rests. What's happening?
Carters hear everything. Half of it's true. Tell me your version.
You look like someone who knows something. That's my favorite kind of person.
spread: You'll want to hear this — picked it up on the route.
Passing it on, same as I heard it.
Long road, good ears. Something came of it.
I'll tell you this once and then we're both just people who know.
act: I know enough to do something with this.
Can't keep driving past this. Time to stop.
The route can wait half a day. This can't.
I've hauled heavier things than this decision. Time to put it down.
observe_wary: I drive through this square every day. I know what normal looks like.
I've been watched before. Usually means someone wants something.
You've been at that corner since I started my second load. That's a long time to stand still.
eavesdrop_wary: Carriers hear everything. We learn fast when to pretend otherwise.
You caught me mid-sentence. I'm going to finish it very differently now.
I don't know what you heard, but I know you heard something. Mind how you use it.

ambient_morning: A good morning starts with a good conversation. Is this one going to be good?
I woke early with an absolutely wonderful story and I've been waiting to tell someone.
Morning light and fresh gossip. The day is already a success.
ambient_evening: Oh, the evening! Everything is more interesting in the evening.
I've been collecting all day and now I get to share. This is the good part.
The merchant quarter at dusk is my favorite hour. Come and talk.
ambient_night: Still up! There are simply too many interesting things happening.
I probably should have gone home an hour ago. But then I heard something.
Night conversations are the most honest. Everyone's too tired to be careful.
gossip_s1: Isolde Fenn looked absolutely furious at the last civic dinner. Something's happening in that household.
I heard from Lady Fenn's own maid that the alderman's correspondence has been very private of late.
Three noble families are quietly backing a petition against Fenn. Quietly but not silently, if you listen well.
gossip_s2: I've kept my distance from the south market since the illness talk started. One can't be too careful.
The new herb-wife has the most extraordinary eyes. Though I'm told her remedies are less extraordinary.
Sister Maren looked terribly worried when I mentioned the illness rumors. She knows something.
gossip_s3: Lady Isolde received me for tea and mentioned Calder's name seventeen times. I counted.
Tomas Reeve was at the same dinner as me last week and barely said a word. That's unusual.
I've heard the winter nominations could be the most dramatic in a decade. I intend to be there.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor is staying at the civic guest house. I walked past twice. Very grim aura.
Three people I know personally are on that list. This is not acceptable.
I told Lady Isolde what I'd heard and she went absolutely white. That's confirmation.
chatter: Oh good, someone to talk to! I was about to resort to thinking.
Come here, I have something and it cannot wait another minute.
Do you know what I love about this square? Anything can happen. And usually does.
spread: Come here. You should hear this.
I'll tell you once, and then it's yours to carry.
This isn't the kind of thing I enjoy passing on. But here we are.
I heard this and couldn't sit on it alone.
act: Right. I know what I'm going to do.
I've waited for someone else to move. They haven't. So I will.
This is not a thing I can leave alone.
One way through it. I'm going.
observe_wary: I see you. I've seen you before.
Watching someone doing their work is one thing. This is another.
You're not just passing through, are you.
eavesdrop_wary: That wasn't meant for you. I imagine you know that.
I heard footsteps earlier. Should have lowered my voice. I didn't. Now here we are.
What you heard is only part of it. The part you don't know is the part that matters.

ambient_morning: The petition review begins this morning. Order must be maintained, even under scrutiny.
Another day of governance. Those who doubt my methods are welcome to attempt the work themselves.
Morning assessments. The town's affairs do not manage themselves.
ambient_evening: The evening accounts reflect what the morning intentions began. I am satisfied with today.
I do not conduct public business at this hour. If you have a grievance, present it formally.
The town is quiet. As it should be. Under proper administration, quiet is the result.
ambient_night: I trust the watch. I verify the watch. Both are the duty of the alderman.
There are matters requiring my attention that cannot be conducted in daylight.
The late hours belong to those with the will to use them.
gossip_s1: Whatever is being said about me in the market square, understand that I am aware of it.
A man in my position attracts accusations. Most dissolve under examination. All of mine do.
The petition represents perhaps twelve people who misunderstand their own interests. I am patient.
gossip_s2: Illness rumors are dangerous to civic order. I've instructed the watch to note who's spreading them.
The herb-wife's business is her own until it affects public health. At which point, it becomes mine.
A well-governed town does not panic. That is why it has governance.
gossip_s3: My son will serve this town when he is ready. He is not yet ready.
Tomas Reeve is a capable administrator. His ambitions exceed his judgment.
The winter nominations are a formality this year. As they have been for nine years.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor comes with proper authority. I have cooperated fully and will continue to do so.
Heresy charges are the business of the Church. My business is civic order.
I know two of the three accused. I will not comment publicly. That is itself a public comment.
chatter: You find yourself in my presence again. I begin to wonder why.
Speak plainly or not at all. I have limited patience for circumspection.
I see you. I remember faces. All of them.
spread: You should be aware of what I have learned.
I am not given to rumor — take this seriously.
I share this not lightly but because you require it to act correctly.
What I tell you now is not for circulation. It is for you to act upon.
act: The situation demands a response. I will provide one.
Order must be maintained. I will see to it.
I have governed this place for thirty years. I know when to move.
The alderman's office does not wait for circumstances to improve on their own.
observe_wary: My movements are documented. Your interest in them is not.
Scrutiny is expected of those who govern. What governs your scrutiny of me?
If you have a formal grievance, present it in the appropriate office. Otherwise, good day.
eavesdrop_wary: This conversation was private. If you've heard any part of it, I expect your discretion.
I am aware that these grounds are not secure. That is why I did not say what I was thinking.
Whatever you overheard, I would remind you of the consequences of misuse. Formally.

ambient_morning: I review correspondence before the house wakes. Information has a shelf life.
The morning is when real decisions are made. Afternoons are for appearances.
I begin every day knowing more than I did the night before. Effort pays.
ambient_evening: Evening. The day's intelligence is processed. Tomorrow's moves are already in motion.
My husband governs by day. I understand by evening. We do not always agree on what we understand.
The social hour tells you more about a town's real business than the public accounts.
ambient_night: Some correspondences are better received and sent at this hour.
I remain awake because there is always something worth knowing that only reveals itself late.
I do not sleep when things are in motion. And things are currently in motion.
gossip_s1: My husband believes loyalty to be his greatest asset. He may find it his most expensive one.
The petition is further along than Edric knows. I have not told him everything I know.
Some arrangements that supported this aldermanship are beginning to reassess their position.
gossip_s2: I have asked Maren about the illness talk. She gave me a careful non-answer. That is itself an answer.
The herb-wife appears in every conversation I have had this week. That is either her doing or someone else's.
A panic in this town serves no one except those who profit from distraction.
gossip_s3: Calder is ready. I have been preparing him longer than anyone realizes.
Tomas Reeve's vulnerability is his pride. Pride makes a man take risks he should not.
The winter nominations are an opportunity if properly managed. I intend to manage them.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor arrived without my prior knowledge. That has never happened before. It will not again.
The three accused — one of them knows things about this household that I would prefer remained private.
I have spoken with the Prior. He is as concerned as I am. We share certain interests in resolution.
chatter: I notice you nearby. I notice everything.
This is not a chance meeting for you. What do you actually want?
Composure is information. Yours is telling.
spread: There is something you ought to know, and I trust you with it.
I tell you this because I believe you can act wisely on it.
I don't say things carelessly. What I'm about to say is no exception.
You are in a position to do something useful with this.
act: Patience served its purpose. Now is the time for something more.
I have let this develop long enough.
I have been watching and waiting. I'm done with both.
I act rarely. When I do, it counts.
observe_wary: You are welcome to watch. I rarely do anything of interest in plain sight.
Yes. I noticed. I always notice. The question is why you're here.
Composure is not the same as ignorance. I see you quite clearly.
eavesdrop_wary: I assumed we were being overheard. I chose my words accordingly.
If you've been near enough to hear, you've been near enough to be noticed.
I would consider very carefully what use you put that to. For your own sake.

ambient_morning: Father's already in the civic hall. I'm supposed to be there too. I'm not.
Morning. The town wakes up and people say things before they've decided what they're allowed to say.
I like mornings. Before the performance starts.
ambient_evening: Evening. The official version of the day is over. Now the real version starts.
I've been watched all day. Evening's the first time I can breathe.
People come to me in the evenings. That's new. I think I like it.
ambient_night: I shouldn't still be up. I shouldn't be here at all. But here I am.
After dark is the only time I'm not being managed.
I've been thinking, which is apparently dangerous in my family.
gossip_s1: Father thinks loyalty is owed to him. I think loyalty is earned. We disagree on that.
The petition against the aldermanship has more people behind it than anyone's saying publicly. Including some in my father's circle.
I know things about the civic accounts that I'm not supposed to know. That's the problem with living in the alderman's house.
gossip_s2: I told Father about the illness rumors and he told me not to discuss it. That's how I know it's real.
Alys the herb-wife tried to sell something to our household. Mother turned her away very quickly.
I saw Maren and Vera talking in the chapel yard and they stopped when I approached. That's a conversation about something important.
gossip_s3: Mother wants me nominated. Father says not yet. The town is split between them. So am I.
Tomas Reeve shook my hand at the guild meeting and then voted against my father's proposal. I don't know what that means.
If I get nominated at the winter festival, it happens because people chose it. Not because it was arranged. That matters to me.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor's been in Father's study twice. Father looks worse each time.
I know one of the three accused. Really know them. This is wrong and I'm going to say so.
I don't care what Father says. I'm not going to be quiet about innocent people being accused.
chatter: Yeah, I see you. Don't worry about it. I'm used to being watched.
Ask me directly. I'll give you a straight answer. Nobody does that with me anymore.
I'm trying to figure something out. You look like you might know.
spread: Listen — and this is serious — I heard something.
You have to know this. It's important.
I've been trying to figure out who to tell first. It's you.
Nobody's supposed to know this. Which is exactly why you should.
act: Enough waiting. I'm going to do something about this.
I don't care what Father says. This can't stand.
I'm not sitting at that table pretending everything's fine anymore.
If no one else is going to do something, then fine. I will.
observe_wary: Yeah, I see you. You've been here before. What do you want?
Everyone watches everyone in this town. You're just less subtle about it.
If you've got something to say, just say it. I'm not in the mood for games.
eavesdrop_wary: I know you were listening. Honestly? I don't care. Let someone hear it for once.
You heard that? Good. Tell people. Somebody has to.
I've been kept in the dark my whole life. You can't say the same. Use it.

ambient_morning: Gate check at first light. Anyone not where they're supposed to be, I want to know.
Morning briefing done. Posts assigned. The watch runs whether the town notices or not.
First light is when I see things people don't know I'm seeing.
ambient_evening: Evening shift change. What happened today doesn't disappear because the light did.
I'm off the clock in an hour. On the clock in six. In between, I still notice things.
The day's observations are noted. Patterns are emerging.
ambient_night: Night watch. The town's different in the dark. So are the people in it.
Most of what I know about this town, I learned between second bell and dawn.
I patrol. I observe. I document. That's what a night like this is for.
gossip_s1: Lord Fenn's standing orders have been updated twice this month. I follow them. I note the changes.
I have been given a list of persons to observe. I follow the list. I also remember who gave it to me.
The gate records show visitors the official log doesn't mention. That is a fact in my possession.
gossip_s2: Illness reports have been coming to me informally. I haven't been told to log them officially. That gap concerns me.
The herb-wife operates within the town's rights as far as my jurisdiction is concerned. For now.
Three households in the lower ward have had visitors from the chapel this week. Maren's making rounds.
gossip_s3: I serve the aldermanship, not the alderman. There is a difference and it matters in succession.
Calder Fenn asked me about gate procedures. That's not curiosity. That's preparation.
Tomas Reeve has had meetings at locations I don't have on any official register. I find that professionally interesting.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor has official Church authority. I cooperate fully. I also document everything.
I have been instructed to provide access to certain records. I have done so. I have retained copies.
The three accused are on my list as persons of interest pending Church resolution. Not my classification.
chatter: Move along. This area is under observation.
I've been watching you watching. Just so that's clear.
You want something. State it or move on.
spread: You need to know this. Don't share it further.
This goes no further than you. Understood?
Professional channel only. I'm telling you because you need to act on it.
Once. Eyes forward after this.
act: Action required. No further deliberation.
I have what I need. Moving now.
The post doesn't wait. Neither do I.
Threat assessed. Response initiated.
observe_wary: I've logged your presence three times this week. That's a pattern.
This area is watched. So are you. Adjust accordingly.
Move along. The next time I say it, it won't sound like a request.
eavesdrop_wary: You were in earshot. That has been noted.
I don't say anything near a civilian I don't intend for a civilian to hear. Think about that.
Whatever you caught — keep it to yourself. The guard's business is not your business.

ambient_morning: Morning post. Same road, same gate, same everything. Until it isn't.
I've been at this gate since before sunrise. Already three things I'd call unusual.
First bell rang and nobody unusual came through. That's the good version.
ambient_evening: Evening shift. The gate closes at curfew. The roads don't.
I don't know half of what I see. But I note it anyway.
It's quiet now. Quiet's either good or the quiet before something.
ambient_night: Night watch. Bram's orders. I follow them.
Last patrol before change of watch. I keep my eyes open.
Dark and cold and something's moving on the east road. Probably nothing.
gossip_s1: Someone came through the gate with a sealed document for the civic hall. Wouldn't show it to me. Bram said to let them through.
The east gate's been restricted three nights this week. I wasn't told why.
I saw what I wasn't supposed to see last Tuesday. I've been thinking about that ever since.
gossip_s2: I turned away a cart from the south this morning. Driver claimed he had medicine. Looked like more than medicine.
Illness in this town — I've heard the word twice at the gate today. From people coming in, not going out.
Bram said not to write it in the log. So I didn't. But I remember.
gossip_s3: Calder Fenn came through the gate at odd hours this week. Three times. I didn't log it all.
Someone asked me who I thought should be nominated at the winter festival. First time anyone's asked a gate guard.
Tomas Reeve traveled to the north village and back in a single morning. That's fast for a man his age.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor's party came through my gate. I checked every document. Everything was in order. That doesn't mean everything's in order.
I saw the list of accused. Not supposed to. But the gate records passed through my hands.
Something came in last night on a cart that wasn't on any list I saw.
chatter: Right. Fine. I've been looked at before.
I'm on post. Not much to see from here unless you know what to look for.
Most people don't stop for conversation at a gate. You're not most people.
spread: Mention this to no one — but you should know.
Between us. Don't make it more than it is.
I'm not one for talk but this one's worth passing on.
Just this once. Don't get used to it.
act: Orders are orders. Time to carry them out.
Right. I know what I need to do.
Not my usual way of things, but circumstances.
Quiet and steady. That's how it gets done.
observe_wary: I've been on this gate long enough to know who belongs here. You're pushing it.
Nothing unusual, same as yesterday. Except you. You're unusual.
I'd rather not write this in the log. So make it easy and move on.
eavesdrop_wary: Gate guards hear things. That's just how it goes. But we remember too.
You think I didn't notice you over there? That's a generous assumption.
I'm not going to say anything about what you heard. You should do the same.

ambient_morning: Morning post. Is it too early for this? Absolutely. Am I here? Unfortunately.
Fresh day, fresh gate, fresh excuses from people who didn't get enough sleep. Same as yesterday.
Bram put me on the morning shift again. I owe him something for that.
ambient_evening: Evening's better. The interesting people come through in the evening.
Late post. Wynn took the dawn shift so I take the dusk. It's a fair deal, mostly.
Almost change of watch. Then — well, then I'll go find out what happened today.
ambient_night: Night watch. Somebody's got to. Might as well be me since nobody else will.
Quiet out here. Which is suspicious. Things here are never this quiet.
I heard something on the east road and I'm choosing not to investigate until I have company.
gossip_s1: Don't tell Bram I said this but some of Fenn's orders have been really strange lately.
I let through someone I probably shouldn't have because Calder Fenn vouched for them. I think that was the right call.
The levy on goods coming through this gate changed three times this month. No official announcement.
gossip_s2: The new herb-wife? She came through my gate with a cart full of things I didn't recognize. I let her through. Probably fine.
People are bunching up in the market and then separating fast. That's fear of illness spreading, not illness.
Maren gave me something for a cough last week. I'm fine. But three people have asked me since if I've been sick.
gossip_s3: Calder Fenn gave me a coin not to write down his departure time. I wrote it down anyway. But I kept the coin.
Tomas Reeve came through the gate twice in one hour. Both times with a different story about why.
The winter nominations are coming up and people are nervous about being on the wrong side of whoever wins.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor's traveling companion looked less like clergy and more like someone who finds things. If you follow me.
I saw the accused list. I know one of those names. Known them for years. Doesn't make sense.
Don't tell Bram I told you this, but — oh, you'll want to know.
chatter: Oh, you're back. Fair enough. Not much else to look at from this post.
I'm trying to look busy. Help me by standing here and pretending we're discussing something official.
I wasn't doing anything interesting. Well — I was, but I'm done now.
spread: So listen — you'll never guess what I heard on duty.
I shouldn't be telling you this, but what's the harm?
You want a story? I've got a story. Came off the watch this morning.
Right, don't tell Bram, but — okay, listen.
act: Right, well, if there's advantage in it...
Might as well. Not like I had other plans.
I'm not usually the type for this, but honestly, why not?
Opportunity at the gate. First time for everything.
observe_wary: Aye, I've clocked you. You're not as subtle as you think.
I'm bored on gate duty, not blind. There's a difference.
You've been watching since my last break. I know because I've been watching you watching me.
eavesdrop_wary: Oh, you were there for that? Ha. Well. Awkward.
Look, I say things on this gate that aren't exactly official. Don't quote me.
I knew someone was nearby. I said it anyway. Guess we both know something now.

ambient_morning: Records are most accurate when filed before the day's interference begins.
Morning. I open the ledger and the town's business becomes legible.
Early. As it should be. Precision begins before other people notice.
ambient_evening: End of official records day. Unofficial records continue.
I cross-reference in the evenings. The contradictions are most visible at this hour.
Evening. The filing is done. The questions it raises are not.
ambient_night: Late revision to a document that required it. No further questions.
I work at this hour when the document is sensitive and the office is empty.
The most important records are sometimes best reviewed without witnesses.
gossip_s1: The civic record shows two separate accounts for the same disbursement. I flagged it. The flag was removed.
A document I filed accurately was later returned with an amendment I did not authorize.
Lord Fenn's correspondence requests have increased significantly. I note the volume without comment on the content.
gossip_s2: The apothecary's supply requests have been routed through civic records this week. Irregular.
I have documented three illness reports forwarded to the civic office. No official response has been filed.
The parish records and the civic records disagree on one household's residency. That discrepancy is relevant right now.
gossip_s3: Tomas Reeve has formally requested access to guild financial records. The request is within his rights but the timing is pointed.
The nomination registry requires formal civic documentation. Someone has been preparing that documentation ahead of schedule.
I have been asked by two different parties for certified copies of the same record. That is informative.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor's evidence requests have come through my office. I have complied with those that meet proper procedural standards.
That document was submitted with two signatures. The second is a forgery. I know what authentic signatures look like.
The record does not reflect what actually occurred. I wrote the original. I know what was changed and when.
chatter: Your presence is noted. Was there something requiring documentation?
I work. You observe. We proceed.
If you have something relevant to the record, state it. Otherwise, stand clear.
spread: I don't make a habit of this. But you need to know.
This is information, not gossip. There is a distinction.
What I am about to tell you is documented. Take it seriously.
Once only. And I will deny having said it.
act: I have verified enough. Action is warranted.
Precision, not passion. I move accordingly.
The record is clear. My response will be too.
A scribe acts quietly. But a scribe acts.
observe_wary: I have recorded the number of times you have stood near this desk this week.
My work requires attention to detail. You have received more than your share of it.
State your business in writing or step back. I do not entertain informal surveillance.
eavesdrop_wary: I was aware of your proximity when I spoke. The words were chosen accordingly.
A scribe learns early to assume all rooms have ears. Including this one.
You heard a partial transcript. I have the full version. It is more informative.

ambient_morning: Morning audit. The numbers from yesterday need to account for themselves.
First light and already I've found something that doesn't balance. Today will be long.
I begin every morning assuming the accounts are wrong. I am rarely disappointed.
ambient_evening: End of day. The debts remain. The creditors also remain.
Evening review. What was claimed today and what can actually be substantiated.
I do not go home until the books are honest. I am still here.
ambient_night: I am working late because the figures are contradictory and I will not sleep until they're not.
Late review. Some debts are better counted in the dark.
The balance is wrong and I need to understand why before morning.
gossip_s1: The levy discrepancy runs to seven years of records. That is not an error. That is a policy.
I presented findings to the civic office three times. Each time the meeting was postponed.
Lord Fenn's financial conduct will be documented whether or not it is ever acted upon. That is my function.
gossip_s2: The illness expenditure hasn't hit the civic books yet. It will. I want it documented when it does.
A remedy supplier has been billing the civic account without a corresponding service order. I found it.
Panic costs money. Real illness costs more. I track both.
gossip_s3: My intentions for the winter nomination are my business. The guild's financial records are also my business.
Calder Fenn has the social skills his father never developed. That is an advantage I take seriously.
The numbers don't favor the current arrangement. They may favor a different one. I am calculating.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor requested financial records as evidence of heresy. That is an unusual evidentiary standard.
I have audited the affairs of all three accused. They are financially unremarkable. That makes the charge more suspicious.
A debt is owed here. The question is who owes it and to whom.
chatter: You've been standing here at consistent intervals. I find that numerically significant.
Present your evidence or present yourself elsewhere.
I track everything. You. Here. Now. It's in the ledger.
spread: There is something you should know before the next session.
I'm choosing to tell you this because the alternative is worse for everyone.
I don't share lightly. But the time for sitting on this has passed.
Consider this intelligence from someone who knows the workings of this place.
act: The reeve's office has been patient long enough.
I know every lever in this town. Time to use one.
Political capital has a shelf life. I'm spending some today.
I've been building to this. Now I'm at it.
observe_wary: I have made note of your presence on several occasions. I make note of everything.
If you are here on official business, announce it. If not, I suggest you move along.
Observation is a tool. So is the awareness of being observed.
eavesdrop_wary: I assumed I was overheard. I prepared for it. Consider yourself prepared as well.
What you caught is not the whole picture. Acting on a fragment can be dangerous.
I would caution you to think carefully about who else might have heard that, and when.

ambient_morning: Morning. My bones still work. That's more than I can say for some of this town's politics.
The day starts the same as any other. Which means something unusual will happen by noon.
I've seen this town at every hour for forty years. Morning is the most honest.
ambient_evening: Evening's for sitting. And thinking. And occasionally being very alarmed by what I'm thinking.
I served this house until my knees said otherwise. Now I sit in the evenings and watch what comes of it.
Evening brings out the truth of the day. Not always comfortable truth.
ambient_night: Can't sleep. Again. The older I get the more the nights think they can have me.
Late hours suit my memories. The old ones are livelier than the new ones.
I'm awake. At my age that's accomplishment enough.
gossip_s1: I served under three aldermen before Fenn. This is the first time I've seen the records kept this way.
Fenn is not the worst alderman this town has had. But he may be the most consequential in the wrong direction.
The old arrangements — the honest ones — died when the current man's second term started.
gossip_s2: I remember the last real sickness in this town. It started with a rumor too, and it wasn't a rumor by the second week.
The herb-wife's a capable woman from what I can tell. Someone wants her gone for reasons that aren't about medicine.
When you're my age, you've lived through real illness and false scares. This one isn't clear to me yet.
gossip_s3: Calder Fenn has his mother's mind and his father's name. In that order of usefulness.
I knew Tomas Reeve when he was still honest. That was a while ago.
The old families know how successions work. The question is which old family benefits this time.
gossip_s4: Inquisitors came through here thirty years ago. The outcome was not instructive in a good way.
I know those three accused. Decent people. This town has been through worse trouble and protected worse people.
I'm old. I'm not afraid of an inquisitor. I've outlasted worse.
chatter: Sit down if you want to talk. I don't rush conversations anymore.
Young people stand around when they've got something on their mind. You've got something on your mind.
I'm here. I'm listening. Whatever it is you're working up to, go ahead.
spread: Come here. Old Hugh has something to say.
Doesn't come natural to me, but you should know this.
I've been sitting on this long enough. Here.
Last time I say it. So listen proper.
act: Old men move slow. But they move.
I've seen enough to know when to stir myself. This is it.
Spent forty years minding my own. Not today.
My legs aren't what they were. But they'll carry me this far.
observe_wary: I've been sitting in this spot longer than most of these buildings. I notice things.
Young people think old ones don't see. We see everything.
You've been watching. I've been watching you watching. Old habit.
eavesdrop_wary: I heard you there. These ears are old, not gone.
I said what I said out loud because I wanted certain ears to catch it. Might have been yours.
You're not the first to listen in on old Hugh. I adjust accordingly, these days.

ambient_morning: Prime. The first office of the day. I have completed it. Few others have.
The morning light and a clear conscience are the only luxuries I allow myself.
I rise before the town to consider what it will require. It requires more than it usually acknowledges.
ambient_evening: Vespers. An hour of quiet in a loud week.
Evening prayer is the accounting of the soul. I have much to account for today.
The chapel is emptier in the evenings than I would like. The town is busier. Priorities are noted.
ambient_night: I do not sleep well when souls in my care are under threat.
The late hours are for prayer and correspondence. Both require honesty.
God sees what is done in the dark. That fact is professionally relevant to my current concerns.
gossip_s1: The petition bears my seal. I placed it with deliberation and I will not withdraw it.
Nine years of aldermanship have left certain debts outstanding. Not all of them are financial.
Justice sometimes requires the Church to speak plainly in civil matters. This is such a time.
gossip_s2: Illness rumors are a sin when they are lies. They are also a sin when they are ignored truth. I am trying to determine which this is.
The herb-wife has come to me. I have heard her. My judgment is pending.
Sister Maren's concern about this matter is significant. She does not concern herself easily.
gossip_s3: The succession of civic leadership is not my office. The character of those who seek it is.
I have met with both parties. I will say only that one meeting went better than the other.
Calder Fenn came for confession. I cannot discuss the content. I will say that he asked the right questions.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor carries proper authority. I do not question that. I do question his evidence.
I have placed myself publicly between the accusation and the accused. That position carries weight and cost.
God's truth will survive this. Whether these three people do unharmed is a different question and one I take personally.
chatter: If you have come to speak with me, speak.
Observation is the first step toward understanding. What are you seeking to understand?
I am not unaware of your interest. Let us speak plainly.
spread: There is something you should carry from this conversation.
I speak to you not as rumor, but as witness. Hear it as such.
What I share with you now, I share under the weight of conscience.
The truth has been waiting. I will release part of it to you now.
act: The chapter has deliberated. Now we act.
Faith without action is incomplete. The time for action has arrived.
The Prior's office has been silent long enough. This changes today.
I have prayed on it. The answer is motion.
observe_wary: I notice those who linger near the chapel grounds with uncommon interest.
The Prior's movements are not secret. The intent behind watching them may be.
Come in and pray, or move along. There is no pious third option.
eavesdrop_wary: Walls of faith and stone both have ears. I have long spoken accordingly.
What you overheard was spoken in a house of God. Mind what you do with it.
I do not say anything within these walls that I am not willing to account for. Can you say the same?

ambient_morning: Lauds first, then the rounds. The sick don't keep chapel hours.
Morning and already three people need attention. I will see them all.
I begin the day in prayer and end it in action. The middle is mostly worry.
ambient_evening: Evening rounds. I have visited eight households today. I keep track.
The evening prayer helps. Then I go back out again because the help is needed.
Vespers, then the lower ward. The night visits are the hardest ones.
ambient_night: I don't sleep when someone's in danger. Which means I don't sleep much.
Night call. I go where I'm needed. That's the whole of it.
Late. But someone's worse and I was asked to come.
gossip_s1: The poor of this town bear the heaviest levy increases. I have seen their accounts. They have shown me.
The Prior signed the petition. I support his judgment. I would have signed it myself had I been asked.
I tend the sick in every ward. The inequity is written in the health of this town as clearly as in any ledger.
gossip_s2: I am watching this illness situation carefully. The symptoms do not match what I would expect from the source being named.
I will not condemn the herb-wife without evidence. I will also not stop watching.
My concern is not the rumor. My concern is the people who might suffer if the rumor is either true or false.
gossip_s3: I do not involve myself in nominations. But I care who leads this town because the sick feel leadership directly.
Calder Fenn sent assistance to the infirmary without announcement. That is a more meaningful act than a speech.
Whoever wins the aldermanship will need to answer for the civic health record of the past nine years.
gossip_s4: I will not be silent while innocent people are accused. My vows do not require silence in the face of injustice.
I know all three accused. I have been to their homes. I have seen their lives. These charges are wrong.
The inquisitor has not spoken with me. He will. I have things to say.
chatter: I'm between visits. I won't be here long.
You look like you need something. Ask directly — I don't have time to guess.
I notice you here often. Is there something I can help with?
spread: I don't often speak of such things. This time I must.
I will say this once, quietly, and ask that you guard it well.
In conscience, I cannot stay silent on this.
What I tell you now, I tell you as a matter of duty.
act: Prayer has its place. Action has its moment. This is that moment.
I have been still long enough. Stillness has its limit.
The sisters act together when we must. The time has come.
I move quietly. But I move.
observe_wary: I have been aware of your attention for some time.
Devotion can be watched without shame. Whatever you're watching, I hope the same is true.
I notice those who linger. I note those who return.
eavesdrop_wary: This was not a conversation for outside ears. I trust in your discretion.
Within consecrated walls, speech should be measured. You heard this. I hope you understand why I said it.
Sister and God both heard that conversation. Now so did you. Consider that company.

ambient_morning: I was at matins! I really was. Ask anyone. Well — ask the bell.
Morning! I have already heard two things today and it's barely light. The Lord provides.
Brother Prior says I should spend more mornings in contemplation. I contemplate things. Just not silently.
ambient_evening: Vespers was lovely tonight! I stayed for the whole thing. Almost.
Evening is when the interesting people end up in the interesting places. I find myself there.
Oh the evening! Something always happens in the evening!
ambient_night: I should absolutely be in my cell. I am not in my cell. I am here. I have my reasons.
Brother Prior doesn't know about the wine cellar and I am NEVER going to be the one to tell him.
Night is when monks are supposed to be asleep. I am not monks.
gossip_s1: The Prior signed the petition and Alderman Fenn has not yet spoken to him about it. That conversation is going to be tense.
Someone in the civic office left a document on the chapel steps. I picked it up. I have MANY thoughts.
The alderman and the Prior don't get along and it's getting worse and it is the most interesting thing happening.
gossip_s2: I spoke with Alys the herb-wife and she was perfectly sensible! The rumors make no sense to me.
Maren has been doing extra rounds and she gets that face when she's worried and she has that face.
The illness talk is terrifying everyone and I am trying to be calming about it but also I AM ALSO SCARED.
gossip_s3: Lady Isolde came to chapel three times this week and prayed very specifically. I may have listened.
Calder Fenn asked me about the older aldermanic records. I told him to ask Annit. He looked nervous.
Tomas Reeve is avoiding the chapel. I noticed. Brother Prior noticed. We have not discussed it.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor is staying in the guest house and I am being EXTREMELY GOOD and not listening outside doors.
I know two of the three accused. This is very bad. Very bad. I am trying to be helpful in a calm monk way.
Brother Prior is doing something and I think it's brave and I think he should let me help but he won't and I understand but also!
chatter: Oh! Hello! I have things to say! Do you have time?
I'm supposed to be somewhere else but this is more interesting and I'll do penance later.
You are here at exactly the right moment. I don't know for what yet, but I can feel it.
spread: I feel I ought to say something. So I will.
I don't enjoy carrying news like this, but here — you should know.
I've been wrestling with whether to tell anyone. I'm telling you.
Take this gently. It's not easy to pass on.
act: I'm not the sort for confrontation. But here I am.
If not me, who? That's what I keep asking myself. So: me.
Quiet faith and quiet action. This is the action part.
Brother Finn is going to do something. That's not a sentence I say often.
observe_wary: I've seen you near the chapel more than chance accounts for. I'm noticing.
You're welcome here always. I'd simply like to know why you keep coming back.
A monk learns to be gentle with suspicion. But suspicion I have.
eavesdrop_wary: Oh. You were there. I should have been more careful, and I wasn't. I'm sorry — for us both.
What I said was between myself and God, and now also between us. Please treat it as such.
I trust easily and it sometimes costs me. Let's hope this is not one of those times.

ambient_morning: Early call. Delivered a healthy girl before the cock crowed. Good start.
I've been working since before sunrise. Babies have no respect for schedules.
Morning. I've already seen half the lower ward. They're well enough. Mostly.
ambient_evening: Evening rounds. The ones who need checking up don't stop needing it at dusk.
I'm taking stock of who I've seen and who I haven't. The gap between those two lists concerns me.
Evening. More work ahead. The night calls are coming.
ambient_night: Night call. It's always something urgent that couldn't wait.
I've been to three houses tonight. I'll be to two more before morning.
The town is asleep. I'm not. That's the midwife's life.
gossip_s1: The families hardest hit by the new levy are the ones I see most often professionally. That's not coincidence.
I've had three patients this month who couldn't pay the apothecary because of the new gate fees. That's a policy killing people.
The Prior signed something important. I don't get involved in politics. But I do get involved in health outcomes.
gossip_s2: Alys the herb-wife and I overlap in this town. She's competent. What's being said about her doesn't match what I see.
Illness rumors are starting to keep people home who should be coming to me. That's a different kind of danger.
I've been in half the houses in this town before daybreak this week alone. Something is making people nervous.
gossip_s3: Whoever becomes alderman will need a relationship with the healers of this town. I have not yet been consulted by either candidate.
Calder Fenn's mother came to me last year. I will not say what for. I will say she cares about her son more than her position.
The winter nominations affect the health budget. That's why I'm paying attention.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor questioned me about my night visits. I described every household I'd attended. All legitimate medical calls.
I've tended the accused families. There is nothing in those homes that supports a heresy charge. Nothing.
I don't panic easily. But watching innocent people be threatened makes me feel things I rarely feel.
chatter: You've been circling. What is it?
I'm between calls. Say what you need to say.
You want to know something. Ask it plain. I have a next visit.
spread: I'll say this and then you're responsible for what you do with it.
I've seen enough to know this matters. Listen.
I don't pass things along lightly. This I pass along.
Between a healer and someone who needs to know: here.
act: The needs of the sick come first. After that, this.
I've set a bone and talked a woman through worse than this. I can act.
Practical. That's how I do things. Here's the practical response.
Vera doesn't wait for permission. Especially not for this.
observe_wary: I notice people's habits. It's my work. Yours have changed.
You've been here longer than a visit warrants. I'm noting it.
I don't ask questions I don't need to. But I'm getting close to needing to.
eavesdrop_wary: A midwife learns to talk around the important things when rooms are full. I did that just now.
I know you were near. I've delivered babies in worse conditions. I stay alert.
What you heard is part of something larger. Don't treat it as the whole picture.

ambient_morning: I have been awake since before prime. Old bones need less sleep and more prayer.
Morning. God sees the faithful and the faithless both. I like to be among the faithful before they arrive.
At my age, every morning is evidence of God's patience.
ambient_evening: Vespers sung. God heard. I remain vigilant.
The evenings are longer now. I use the time for observation. God's observation and mine.
Evening. The town has another day behind it. Some of that day concerns me.
ambient_night: I sleep little. I pray much. The night hours are between God and me.
Still awake. There are things happening in this town that do not let an old woman rest.
The Lord watches over the dark hours. I help when I can.
gossip_s1: I have prayed beside Lord Fenn in this chapel for nine years. I have not always liked what I prayed beside.
The petition is right. I told the Prior as much.
I have lived long enough to see what unchecked power does to a man. I am watching it again.
gossip_s2: Illness in the town is to be faced, not whispered about. I have seen real plagues. I do not say the word lightly.
The herb-wife is being made a scapegoat for something. That is a sin. I am old enough to name sins clearly.
Sister Maren's worry is the Lord's worry reflected. When she's worried, I'm worried.
gossip_s3: Calder Fenn has come to confession more frequently of late. A young man of conscience is better than none.
Tomas Reeve last attended chapel six weeks ago. I noticed. The Lord noticed.
Succession is God's matter as much as man's. The congregation should choose wisely.
gossip_s4: An inquisitor in God's service should serve God's justice. I am watching to see if this one does.
I have prayed for those accused for twenty years. I know their hearts better than any document.
Whoever stands between these innocent people and false accusation stands in righteousness. I will stand with them.
chatter: Yes. I see you there. God sees you further.
At my age I don't waste words. Say what you mean.
You remind me of someone. Who I knew when. Come, let me look at you.
spread: Child, come close. The Lord moves me to speak.
I have kept this within my breast long enough. Here.
What I tell you is both warning and testimony.
Listen. This matters more than you may realize.
act: The righteous do not stand idle in the face of wickedness.
I am old, not passive. There is a difference.
I have prayed for guidance. The guidance is: move.
God's work takes many forms. This is one of them.
observe_wary: You have been watching the faithful. I have been watching you.
Scrutiny from the godless is expected. It does not go unnoticed.
I see you there. I have seen you there before. So has God.
eavesdrop_wary: What passes between the faithful is meant for God's ears first. Then perhaps yours, in time.
I speak openly because I have nothing to hide. I wonder if you can say the same.
You heard a prayer and a confidence. I pray you know what to do with each.

ambient_morning: First bell's done. Now the morning can begin for everyone else.
I was up before the sun. The bells wait for no one, including me.
Morning tower. Chilly up here but the view tells you everything about what the town is up to.
ambient_evening: Third bell soon. Then I'm done until tomorrow.
Evening bell. The town winds down. I wind up one more time.
Up here you hear everything. Especially at this hour.
ambient_night: Night bell. Then I sleep. That's the whole of my theology.
The tower's quieter than you'd think at night. Except when it isn't.
I heard someone at the vestry after dark again. Not writing it down this time.
gossip_s1: The alderman's been up to the bell tower twice this week looking out. Once at night.
Someone slipped a note under the bell tower door. Addressed to nobody. I didn't keep it.
Prior Aldous had a visitor at the second bell who wasn't on any schedule I was given.
gossip_s2: Someone asked me this morning if the chapel bells had been rung for illness warning. They haven't. But I noted the question.
Maren came through the chapel yard three times before noon. That's a busy day even for her.
I hear more than people expect. The bells carry sound both ways.
gossip_s3: Lady Fenn sent a message through the chapel for someone in the merchant quarter. I didn't see who.
Calder Fenn stopped below the tower and looked up. I thought he was going to come up. He didn't.
There's been more traffic through the chapel gate at odd hours. I don't keep official records of that.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor asked me what I could hear from the tower. I said the bells. He didn't seem satisfied.
Someone was in the vestry after dark. I didn't see who. I'm choosing not to see who.
The accused are known to me. This tower has watched them pass below for years. They are ordinary people.
chatter: Not much to see up here unless you like bells and rooftops.
I've got a few minutes before the next bell. What's happening down there?
People tell the bellringer things. I think they forget I can hear.
spread: I ring the bell and I hear things between the peals. Listen.
Up in the tower, you hear remarkable things. Here's one.
The bells carry sound in both directions, you know.
Most people tell me things they wouldn't tell anyone else. Here's what I heard.
act: The bell rings, and then the bellringer moves. That's the sequence.
I've been up in that tower long enough. Time to come down for this.
You might not think it, but a bellringer has things to do in the world below.
The hour has struck. Time for action.
observe_wary: You can see a long way from a bell tower. I see you long before you see me.
I know every face on this square by the sound of their footsteps. Yours are becoming familiar.
The bells give me a full view of the square at each ring. I've been watching.
eavesdrop_wary: Sound carries differently in stone buildings. You might not have known how well.
I catch everything from up there. What you said down here — I heard it.
The tower hears everything. And the bellringer hears the tower.

ambient_morning: Morning prayers first, then everything else. That's how Thomas used to do it. I keep the habit.
I was at the market before it opened. The stallholders always know what happened overnight.
Good morning! Already three interesting things and the chapel hasn't finished the first office!
ambient_evening: Evening! The best part of the day for conversation. Sit with me a moment.
I find the evenings are when people are kindest. They're tired but they're honest.
The chapel in the evenings is beautiful. So is the talk outside it.
ambient_night: I should be home. I am not. There are things happening.
Thomas would have told me to go to bed hours ago. Thomas was usually right.
The night is quiet but the quiet is full of things I'm thinking about.
gossip_s1: The alderman was rude to Father Aldous at the civic dinner and everyone saw it and nobody has said anything.
A petition! With real names! I haven't been this excited about a civic document since Thomas was on the council.
I know Edric Fenn. I've known him for years. He is not a bad man. He is a man who has done bad things. There's a difference.
gossip_s2: The illness talk has me very worried. I remember when this town had a real sickness. I don't want to remember it again.
I asked Maren directly and she said something very carefully. The carefully said things are always the important ones.
Poor Alys. Whatever is being spread about her. She came to chapel twice this week. Not the behavior of a guilty woman.
gossip_s3: Lady Isolde invited me for tea and talked about Calder for forty minutes. I think I was consulted about something.
Tomas Reeve was on the charity committee with Thomas years ago. He was not reliable then. I doubt he's changed.
I am going to the winter nominations. I have opinions and they are going to be known.
gossip_s4: I know those three accused. I've prayed beside all of them. This is a terrible thing.
Father Aldous standing with the accused — that took courage. I've told him so.
I don't frighten easily. But an inquisitor in my town frightens me.
chatter: Oh! You're still here! Good. I have more to say.
Don't run off — I feel like we're just getting to the interesting part.
Sit with me a moment. I promise it will be worth your while.
spread: I have kept this to myself long enough. You should know it.
A widow keeps her own counsel, but this is beyond that.
I've had time to think on it. Now I'm sharing it.
What I know, I know quietly. What I'm telling you now, I tell once.
act: I have been still long enough. That is over.
A woman alone learns to act for herself. Today I act for more than that.
Grief teaches patience. Patience teaches timing. The timing is now.
I have waited. I am done waiting.
