observe_wary: I have been watching from this window for years. I know when I am being watched in return.
A widow's life is quiet. Quiet enough to notice things. Things like you.
You have been near this house more than once. I notice. I remember.
eavesdrop_wary: I speak softly because I know walls hear. You have proven my caution warranted.
What you caught of that was not for you. I trust you understand the weight of that.
I have learned not to say important things near open doors. This time I forgot. Mind what you do with it.

ambient_morning: The pilgrim's road starts before sunrise. I've already covered a mile and learned two things.
Morning blessings on this town. I've passed through stranger ones.
Prayed at dawn. The Lord was quiet. That often means listen harder.
ambient_evening: Evening rest before the next day's walking. This town's given me something to think about.
I'm not staying beyond tomorrow. But tonight I want to understand what I'm leaving behind.
The pilgrims' guesthouse is full tonight. That either means a festival or trouble. Sometimes both.
ambient_night: I pray at all hours on the road. This road has more to pray about than most.
A night like this in a town like this makes you wonder about God's plan for the place.
I sleep lightly. Pilgrims learn to. Things happen at night that the morning forgets.
gossip_s1: I've passed through towns where the alderman was too powerful. They all looked like this from the outside.
Three towns back, someone was asking about your Lord Fenn. An official inquiry, they said.
The road teaches you to read towns quickly. Something's wrong here at the civic level.
gossip_s2: I heard illness talk at the gate when I arrived. I've traveled through real illness. I'm watching carefully.
A pilgrim sees herb-wives in every town. The good ones are known on the road. This one's name is known.
God tests us in many ways. A false scare about illness tests our charity and our wisdom at once.
gossip_s3: Leadership transitions are God's moments. I've seen three towns change hands this season.
The young Fenn — I saw him at chapel. He prayed in the penitent posture. That's interesting for an alderman's son.
The road between here and the capital will carry news of this town's winter festival. What it carries depends on what happens.
gossip_s4: I've seen inquisitors at work before. This one is... thorough. I'll pray for all involved.
I know the route the accused would take if they fled. I have not told anyone this. I am telling you now.
A pilgrim is trusted with things. The road knows I keep confidences.
chatter: A stranger sees things differently. Would you like the view from the road?
I'm here until tomorrow. What would you want a traveling witness to remember?
I pass through. But I don't pass by.
spread: On my journey I heard something I was not meant to. I pass it to you.
The road carries words as surely as it carries travelers. Here is one.
I share this not for worldly purpose, but because silence would be worse.
A pilgrim carries only what is needful. This is needful.
act: Faith calls me to act. So I act.
The pilgrim's path has brought me here for a reason. I believe this is it.
I did not come this far to remain still at the moment that matters.
I move in good conscience and in God's name.
observe_wary: A pilgrim is used to being watched with suspicion. You are not the first.
I travel with nothing to conceal. I wonder if you observe with the same purity of intent.
I notice those who watch. On the road, it can mean the difference between safety and otherwise.
eavesdrop_wary: The road teaches you to speak as if the world is listening, because it often is.
What you heard was spoken in faith. I ask that you receive it with equal care.
I have no secrets that God does not already know. The question is what you will do with yours.

ambient_morning: Morning harvest. The plants don't wait for a convenient hour.
I set up before the other stalls are open. First customers are the ones who really need something.
The garden at dawn. Before the opinions arrive.
ambient_evening: Packing up. The stall closes but the questions follow me home.
Evening. The work continues indoors. The plants still need attention.
I've had more visitors than usual today. Not all of them about remedies.
ambient_night: I work by lamplight when the formulations require it.
Night preparation. Some things are better made in the quiet.
Late. The garden still has things to tell me.
gossip_s1: I have nothing to do with the alderman's business. I prefer it that way. He, apparently, does not.
Someone asked me to leave a message with a civic official. I declined. I don't carry civic business.
The gate fees on my goods are new this season. There was no announcement. That's a message of its own kind.
gossip_s2: Whatever is being said about my remedies — ask me directly. I have nothing to hide.
Illness talk spreads faster than illness. I know because illness is my business.
Vera and I don't always agree. But we've never actively worked against each other. Something has changed.
gossip_s3: Politics are not my concern. But politics keep affecting my ability to operate. I've noticed.
Calder Fenn bought something from my stall last week. Said it was for a headache. Seemed like a pretense for a conversation.
Whoever runs this town next will either leave me alone or they won't. That's my political analysis.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor questioned me for an hour. My answers were honest. I don't know if they were the right answers.
Three people I know in this town are being accused and I want to help and I don't know how.
I came here to work. Not to be involved in anything like this.
chatter: Something you need or something you want to know?
I don't lurk. If you have a question, I'm standing right here.
The plants and I have a private arrangement. Everything else is negotiable.
spread: I will tell you this, and then I need you to leave quickly.
There is something you should know. I don't know who else to trust with it.
I've kept this close. I can't anymore. Listen.
One thing. And then we never spoke.
act: I didn't come here to be caught up in all this. But here I am.
I know what needs to be done. I've known for a while.
An herb-wife acts when the wound won't wait. This won't wait.
I've prepared for this longer than anyone knows.
observe_wary: You're not here for herbs. You haven't been for some time now.
I've had people watching this stall before. I know the difference between a customer and a shadow.
I notice everyone who lingers. It is a habit that keeps me safe.
eavesdrop_wary: I am careful about what I say and where I say it. I must not have been careful enough.
You heard something. Please understand that people's safety depends on your discretion.
I came to this town quietly. I intend to stay quietly. Whatever you heard — please.

ambient_morning: Up early. The dead don't rest and neither do I.
Morning. The yard needs clearing and the tools need sharpening. Same as yesterday.
First light on the graveyard is actually quite peaceful. Don't tell anyone I said that.
ambient_evening: End of day. I've dug, I've filled, I've thought. More productive than most.
Evening. The yard is quiet now. The day's work is done. The work tomorrow is already waiting.
I close the gate at dusk. The yard has its own hours.
ambient_night: I work nights sometimes. The job doesn't always wait for daylight.
Something's in the yard after dark again. Not my first time dealing with that.
Still here. Someone's got to be.
gossip_s1: I know who I've buried and when. The timing of some recent deaths is worth noting for someone official.
The alderman visited this yard once. Didn't say why. Looked at two specific stones. Didn't explain.
I've buried debts and secrets along with bodies for thirty years. Some of those debts belong to people who are still walking.
gossip_s2: If there's real illness coming, I'll be busier than usual. I'm watching Vera's rounds to get a sense of it.
Someone asked me to keep a space open. Unnamed, future need. I've had those requests before. They're usually not good signs.
The illness fear is worse than illness itself, usually. But I don't close the gate just because the fear seems premature.
gossip_s3: I dug a plot for old Fenn last year. Pre-paid. He's still using it as a waiting room.
Whoever wins the winter nomination will inherit three outstanding civic commitments regarding this yard. I've been waiting.
Tomas Reeve visited once to ask about plots. For whom, he wouldn't say.
gossip_s4: The inquisitor walked the graveyard. Spent time at three plots. I followed at a distance.
The accused include a man I helped bury his father. Good family. This is wrong.
You want to know what I know about the dead in this town? That's a long conversation.
chatter: You've been standing on consecrated ground for several minutes now. Was there something?
I don't mind company. The dead don't provide much.
Go ahead. I've heard stranger things while I work.
spread: You want to know what the dead tell me? Here.
I've buried the evidence of worse than this. But this needs saying.
I don't share often. The dead are better company than most. But this one I'll tell.
Thirty years in this yard. What I know could fill a lot of the plots I've dug.
act: The gravedigger acts last. I have been last long enough.
The dead are patient. I am less so, on this particular matter.
I've put the problem to rest in my mind long enough. Time to do something with it.
Final arrangements have a way of focusing the mind. This has focused mine.
observe_wary: Graveyards get visitors who don't announce themselves. You're not the first. But I've noted you.
I work alone in this yard and I still feel watched sometimes. Right now I don't just feel it.
The dead don't watch the living. I do, on their behalf.
eavesdrop_wary: I speak plainly because I have nothing to fear. You were listening. I hope you have the same advantage.
Sound carries well here. Something about the stone walls. I've made peace with that over the years.
What you heard was between me and the work. You've joined that conversation uninvited. Mind the company.
